pages.name: Home
pages.description: Entry point to the environmental research information site, highlighting key sections and featured content and providing a global cross-content search entry.
primary_functions: Orient users to the purpose of the site and its main content areas (projects, publications, datasets, events).
Provide prominent navigation links to Projects, Publications, Datasets, and Events pages.
Offer a global search bar to jump directly into project, publication, dataset, or event searches.
Show featured or trending projects, publications, and upcoming events as quick-entry cards.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Submit global search and choose the Projects tab or 'See all project results' option.
Submit global search and choose the Publications tab or 'See all publication results' option.
Submit global search and choose the Datasets tab or 'See all dataset results' option.
Submit global search and choose the Events tab or 'See all event results' option.
Click a featured project card.
Click an upcoming events teaser such as 'View all events'.

access_methods.description: Main navigation entry labeled 'Home'.
Directly visiting index.html or the root URL.

pages.name: Projects
pages.description: Search, filter, and browse environmental research projects, including saving them into lists and preparing comparisons.
primary_functions: Search projects by keyword (e.g., freshwater biodiversity, microplastics ocean).
Filter projects by criteria such as topic, region, status, risk level, components/tags, fieldwork year, start year range, budget/funding range, and team size.
Sort project results by options including funding amount, start date, end date, and next milestone.
Open individual project detail pages by clicking project cards.
Save projects to comparison lists using a bookmark or Save icon and create new named lists (e.g., Africa Freshwater 2018-22).
Select multiple projects with an Add to compare control and open the Project Comparison page.

incoming_params.description: Initial keyword query for project search (e.g., 'freshwater biodiversity').
Preselected topic filter (Project.topic enum value, e.g., 'freshwater_biodiversity').
Preselected region filter (Project.region enum value, e.g., 'africa').
Preselected status filter (Project.status enum value, e.g., 'ongoing').
Preselected risk level filter (Project.risk_level enum value, e.g., 'medium').
Comma-separated list of components/tags to filter by (e.g., includes 'Community engagement').
Minimum project start year for range filtering (e.g., 2018).
Maximum project start year for range filtering (e.g., 2022).
Minimum total funding amount (e.g., 1000000).
Maximum total funding amount for upper-bound filters (e.g., 500000).
Preselected team size bucket ID (e.g., '1_10').
Specific fieldwork year to filter projects by (e.g., 2024).
Sort key value (e.g., 'funding_high_to_low', 'next_milestone_soonest_first').
Results page number for pagination.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click a project card or project title in the results list.
Click the 'Compare (n)' button after selecting projects with 'Add to compare'.
Change filters or sorting and apply them; the page updates and URL reflects current state.

access_methods.description: Main navigation entry labeled 'Projects'.
Accessed with pre-configured search and filter state via query parameters.
Users can bookmark or share a filtered/sorted projects view.

pages.name: Project Detail
pages.description: Detailed view of a single project with subscription, contact, note-taking, comparison, list-saving, and briefing creation tools.
primary_functions: Display full project information including objectives, methods, location, timeline, risk level, funding, and team members.
Allow users to subscribe to project updates by entering an email address and opting into email notifications.
Provide a Contact project team form to send messages (e.g., to join a volunteer mailing list).
Enable saving principal investigator (PI) contact details to a personal note, including creating a new note titled (e.g., Collaboration ideas).
Allow users to add the project to a new or existing briefing and choose briefing sections such as Objectives, Methods, and Key outcomes.
Provide quick actions to follow/unfollow the project and to add/remove it from comparison lists.

incoming_params.description: Project ID to display in detail view.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click a 'More projects like this' link based on topic or region.
After clicking 'Add to compare', choose 'View comparison' from a quick-action link.
After using 'Save PI contact' and saving the note, click 'Open in My Notes'.
After creating a briefing with 'Add to briefing', click 'View briefing'.

access_methods.description: Accessed via project_detail.html?id={projectId} from lists, comparisons, notes, or briefings.
Direct links from emails or shared URLs to a specific project.

pages.name: Project Comparison
pages.description: Side-by-side comparison of selected research projects stored in the current comparison session.
primary_functions: Display selected projects in a table comparing key attributes such as duration, funding amount, region, status, and topic.
Highlight differences in project duration to help choose the longer-duration project when needed.
Provide links or buttons on each compared project to open its Project Detail page.
Allow users to adjust which projects are in the comparison (e.g., remove one, return to Projects to add others).

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click a project title or 'Open details' link in the comparison table.
Click 'Back to project search' or 'Add more projects' from the comparison page.

access_methods.description: Accessed when clicking 'Compare (n)' on the Projects page.
May be visited directly (comparison state is restored from the local compare session).

pages.name: Publications
pages.description: Search and browse research publications related to the projects and manage adding them into reading lists.
primary_functions: Search publications by keyword (e.g., PM2.5 urban air quality).
Filter publications by type (such as peer-reviewed article), publication year range, topic, and other relevant facets.
Sort publication results by metrics like citation count, publication date, or relevance.
Add individual publications to a reading list via an Add to reading list action.
Create a new reading list during the add flow (e.g., Urban Air Quality) or add to an existing list.

incoming_params.description: Initial keyword query for publication search (e.g., 'PM2.5 urban air quality').
Preselected publication type filter (Publication.type enum value, e.g., 'peer_reviewed_article').
Minimum publication year for range filters (e.g., 2019).
Maximum publication year for range filters (e.g., 2023).
Sort key value (e.g., 'citations_high_to_low').
Results page number for pagination.
Optional publication ID to highlight or open details for within the results.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click 'Manage reading lists' from the publications toolbar.
After adding items to a reading list, click 'View list'.

access_methods.description: Main navigation entry labeled 'Publications'.
Accessed with pre-configured publication search and filters via query parameters.

pages.name: Reading Lists
pages.description: Overview of all reading lists saved by the user, with tools to create, rename, or delete lists.
primary_functions: Display a list of all user-created reading lists (e.g., Urban Air Quality) with counts of items in each.
Allow users to create new empty reading lists, rename existing lists, or delete lists.
Provide links to open each list in the Reading List Detail page.
Serve as the destination when selecting Reading lists from the header user toolbar or profile menu.

incoming_params.description: Optional reading list ID to highlight after creation or update.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click a reading list name in the overview table.
Click 'Find publications to add' from an empty state or list-level CTA.

access_methods.description: Main navigation entry labeled 'Reading lists'.
Can highlight a particular list using the focus_list_id parameter.

pages.name: Reading List Detail
pages.description: Detailed view of a single reading list and its saved publications with list item management tools.
primary_functions: Display all publications contained in a specific reading list.
Allow users to remove individual publications from the list.
Offer basic organization tools, such as reordering items or adding notes/labels to items (optional).
Provide quick access from each publication entry back to its full record (if needed) on the Publications page.
Allow renaming or deleting the current reading list.

incoming_params.description: ID of the reading list to display.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click a breadcrumb or 'All reading lists' link.
Click 'Search for related publications' for this list.

access_methods.description: Accessed via reading_list.html?reading_list_id={id} from the Reading Lists or Publications pages.
Shared links to a specific reading list detail view.

pages.name: Events
pages.description: Search and browse events such as webinars, workshops, and conferences, and act as the entry point for event registration flows.
primary_functions: Search events by keyword and filter by event type (e.g., Webinar), topic (e.g., Climate adaptation), and region.
Filter events by date range (e.g., 01/01/2025 to 06/30/2025) using calendar or date input controls.
Sort events by date (soonest first), relevance, or other available criteria.
Display event cards with key information and a Register button.
Open individual Event Detail pages for more information and registration when Register or the event title is clicked.

incoming_params.description: Initial keyword query for event search.
Preselected event type filter (Event.event_type enum value, e.g., 'webinar').
Preselected event topic filter (Event.topic enum value, e.g., 'climate_adaptation').
Preselected event region filter (Event.region enum value, e.g., 'south_america').
Start date for the events date range filter in ISO or localized format (e.g., '2025-01-01').
End date for the events date range filter (e.g., '2025-06-30').
Sort key value (e.g., 'date_soonest_first').
Results page number for pagination.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click an event title or the 'Register' button on an event card.

access_methods.description: Main navigation entry labeled 'Events'.
Accessed with pre-configured filters and date range via query parameters.

pages.name: Event Detail
pages.description: Detailed information and registration form for a specific event or webinar, including schedule and 'Add to My Schedule' actions.
primary_functions: Display full event information including title, description, speakers, date and time, and region.
Provide a registration form collecting name and email (e.g., Alex Rivera, alex.rivera@example.org).
Confirm successful registration and show a clear status on the page after submission.
Offer an Add to My Schedule button to add the event to the user9s schedule after registration.
Provide breadcrumb or navigation links to return to the main Events page.

incoming_params.description: ID of the event to display in detail view.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click 'Back to events' breadcrumb or similar navigation control.
After clicking 'Add to My Schedule', choose 'View my schedule'.

access_methods.description: Accessed via event_detail.html?event_id={id} from the Events list or My Schedule.
Direct links to an event detail page from email confirmations or shared URLs.

pages.name: My Schedule
pages.description: Personal schedule of events for which the user has registered and chosen to add to 'My Schedule'.
primary_functions: List all events the user has added to their schedule, with dates and times.
Allow users to open each event in the Event Detail page for more information.
Provide tools to remove events from the schedule if plans change.
Optionally allow exporting or printing the schedule (e.g., as calendar or PDF).

incoming_params.description: Optional event ID to highlight after adding it to the schedule.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click an event title in the schedule list.

access_methods.description: Main navigation entry labeled 'My schedule'.
Can optionally highlight a specific event via highlight_event_id.

pages.name: Datasets
pages.description: Search and browse datasets related to environmental research projects, including adding them to a centralized download queue.
primary_functions: Search datasets by keyword (e.g., deforestation).
Filter datasets by region (e.g., Southeast Asia), license (e.g., CC-BY, CC0), file format (e.g., CSV), topic, and last updated date.
Sort dataset results by last updated date, relevance, or size.
Add datasets to a download queue using an �1cAdd to download queue�1d button.
Open more detailed dataset metadata (if needed) in-line or via expansion within the page.

incoming_params.description: Initial keyword query for dataset search (e.g., 'deforestation').
Preselected dataset topic filter (Dataset.topic enum).
Preselected dataset region filter (Dataset.region enum, e.g., 'southeast_asia').
Comma-separated list of license enum values (e.g., 'cc_by,cc0') to preselect.
Preselected file format filter (Dataset.file_format_enum, e.g., 'csv').
Minimum last-updated date for range filter.
Maximum last-updated date for range filter.
Sort key value (e.g., 'last_updated_newest_first').
Results page number for pagination.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click the header 'Download queue' link or an in-page 'View download queue' CTA.

access_methods.description: Main navigation entry labeled 'Datasets'.
Accessed with pre-configured filters and sort order via query parameters.

pages.name: Download Queue
pages.description: Staging area for datasets the user has selected for download, with options to refine the queue and initiate a combined ZIP download.
primary_functions: Display all datasets currently in the user9s download queue.
Allow users to remove individual datasets from the queue before downloading.
Provide a �1cDownload as ZIP�1d button to prepare and start a combined download of all queued datasets.
Show basic information for each dataset, such as title, region, license, and file format.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click 'Continue browsing datasets' from the download queue page.

access_methods.description: Main navigation entry labeled 'Download queue'.
Linked from the Datasets page whenever items are added to the queue.

pages.name: My Notes
pages.description: Central place for managing personal notes related to projects and contacts, including notes created from project PI contact saves.
primary_functions: List all user-created notes, including those created from project pages (e.g., �1cCollaboration ideas�1d).
Allow users to open and edit existing notes, including title and body text.
Enable creation of new standalone notes not tied to a specific action.
Allow deletion of notes that are no longer needed.
Provide links back to related project detail pages when notes are associated with a project or PI.

incoming_params.description: Optional note ID to open directly in edit mode.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click 'Go to related project' on a note associated with a project.

access_methods.description: Main navigation entry labeled 'Notes'.
Directly open a specific note via notes.html?note_id={id}.

pages.name: Briefings
pages.description: Overview and detail view for project briefings created from selected sections of project content, with editing and export tools.
primary_functions: List all briefings the user has generated from project detail pages.
Display the content of a selected briefing (via a briefingId parameter) including chosen sections such as Objectives, Methods, and Key outcomes.
Allow users to edit included sections or add contextual notes to a briefing.
Provide export or print options for briefings (e.g., PDF or print-friendly view).
Offer navigation back to related project detail pages from within a briefing.

incoming_params.description: Optional briefing ID to open directly in detail view.

outgoing_connections.trigger: From within a briefing, click 'View source project'.

access_methods.description: Main navigation entry labeled 'Briefings'.
Access a specific briefing via briefings.html?briefing_id={id}.

pages.name: About
pages.description: Background information about the environmental research project information site, including mission, scope, data sources, and funders.
primary_functions: Explain the mission, scope, and data sources of the site.
Describe how projects, publications, datasets, and events are curated and updated.
Provide organizational or funder information where relevant.
Link to Help and Policies pages for further details.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click a 'How to use this site' or 'Help' link in the About content.
Click 'Privacy & terms' within the About text.
Click 'Explore all projects' CTA in the About page.

access_methods.description: Footer navigation link labeled 'About'.
Direct access via about.html.

pages.name: Help
pages.description: Support and guidance for using the website effectively, including walkthroughs for all key multi-step tasks and troubleshooting advice.
primary_functions: Provide step-by-step guidance on common tasks (searching projects, creating reading lists, registering for events, downloading datasets).
Offer troubleshooting tips for issues like email subscriptions or downloads.
Provide contact information or a general support email for site-related questions (distinct from project team contacts).
Link to Policies for privacy and terms information.

incoming_params.description: Optional task identifier used to scroll to or highlight a specific help section.

outgoing_connections.trigger: From a 'Search projects' help guide, click 'Try it now'.
From a 'Create a reading list' help guide, click 'Open Publications'.
From a 'Download datasets' help guide, click 'Open Datasets'.
From a 'Register for webinars' help guide, click 'Open Events'.
Click a 'Privacy & terms' link in troubleshooting or support sections.

access_methods.description: Footer navigation link labeled 'Help'.
Direct access via help.html, possibly with a task parameter.

pages.name: Privacy & Terms
pages.description: Legal and policy information for the site, covering privacy, terms of use, and data protection contacts.
primary_functions: Present the site9s privacy policy, including how user data (such as email addresses for subscriptions and registrations) is handled.
Outline terms of use for the website and its content.
Describe licensing and usage rights for datasets and publications where relevant.
Provide contact details for policy or data protection inquiries.

incoming_params.description: Optional anchor specifying which policy section to scroll to (e.g., 'privacy' or 'terms').

access_methods.description: Footer navigation link labeled 'Privacy & terms'.
Direct access via policies.html, including deep-linking to sections.

header_links.description: Browse and filter environmental research projects
Find and queue datasets for download
Browse and register for events and webinars
View and manage your registered events
View and edit your saved notes (e.g., collaboration ideas)
View and manage generated project briefings

footer_links.description: About this environmental research information site
How to use the site and get support
Privacy policy and terms of use
